nativeRange.habitat: Cryptocoryne walkeri is native to streams and shaded slow-moving freshwater bodies in Sri Lanka, typically growing along the margins of rainforest streams where it experiences seasonal transitions between submerged and partially exposed growth.

avoidWith: Silver Dollars and other plant-eating fish

summary: Walker's Water Trumpet is a compact rosette plant from Sri Lanka, prized for its wavy-edged green to olive-brown leaves and undemanding care. It tends to stay smaller than other commonly kept crypts, making it a natural choice for midground planting.
careNotes: Cryptocoryne walkeri is a compact rosette plant native to streams in Sri Lanka, closely related to Cryptocoryne wendtii but generally staying smaller and more uniformly sized in the aquarium (typically 10-20 cm tall, with the dwarf 'lutea' form sometimes reaching only about 10-15 cm). Leaves are lance-shaped with a wavy/slightly rippled margin and range from bright green to olive- or bronze-brown depending on light intensity and form — higher light tends to produce more brown/bronze coloration, while shaded conditions favor green. As with all crypts, it is a heavy root feeder — plant it in nutrient-rich aquarium soil and supplement with root tabs near the root zone for best results. Plant by burying the roots up to (but NOT including) the crown where the leaves emerge from the rhizome; burying the crown causes rot. CRYPT MELT WARNING: Cryptocoryne walkeri is well known for crypt melt — when newly planted, after a water-change shock, or after any major parameter shift, the plant frequently sheds most or all of its existing leaves over a few days. This is a normal acclimation response; do NOT pull up the rhizome and roots — the plant typically regrows new leaves adapted to the new conditions within 2-4 weeks. To minimize melt, keep parameters stable and avoid moving an established plant once it has settled. Propagates by runners (stolons) that produce daughter plants alongside the parent; these can be cut from the runner and replanted once they have several leaves and their own roots.
breedingNotes: Propagated primarily by runners (stolons) that produce daughter plants alongside the parent; once the daughter plant has developed several leaves and a healthy root system, it can be carefully separated from the runner and replanted. Rhizome division is also possible: cut a healthy rhizome into sections, each retaining leaves and roots, and replant. Like other crypts, Cryptocoryne walkeri can flower (producing the spathe-and-spadix inflorescence typical of Araceae) when grown emersed, but seed propagation is not practical in the home aquarium.

primary.name: Buce Plant
primary.notes: Used as primary because Tropica does not maintain a dedicated species page for Cryptocoryne walkeri (the assigned primary URL https://tropica.com/en/search?q=Cryptocoryne+walkeri returns no species hit). Buce Plant confirmed Sri Lankan origin, pH 5.5-8.0, slow growth, midground placement, runner propagation, height 4-6 inches (~10-15 cm), low-to-moderate light, optional CO2, melt warning, and quality-aquarium-soil recommendation.

additional.name: Nature Aquariums USA
additional.notes: Cross-checked Sri Lankan origin, pH 5.5-8.0, slow growth rate, 4-6 inch height (~10-15 cm), midground placement, runner propagation, low-to-moderate lighting, optional CO2, beginner difficulty, and melt warning under unstable parameters.

additional.name: Aquarium Lesson
additional.notes: Authoritative source for taxonomy (Araceae, Alismatales) and water parameters: temperature 22-26 °C, pH 5-8, kH 1-18 dKH, rosette form, slow growth, foreground-to-midground placement, low light demand, propagation by daughter plants. Profile is specifically for the 'lutea' variety, which is sometimes treated as a synonym of C. walkeri.

additional.name: Aqua Imports
additional.notes: Cross-checked beginner care level, low-to-moderate lighting, and 'medium to large plant usually used for mid- or background planting' (consistent with the 10-20 cm height range).

additional.name: Aqua-Fish.net Cryptocoryne care guide
additional.notes: General Cryptocoryne care reference used for cross-checking crypt-melt behavior, runner propagation, and heavy-root-feeder husbandry shared across the genus.
